content: After spending 2 years building AI agents for GTM teams, I’ve learned a lot about how to get AI to work across a team. To build a good AI workflow you need these four things: 1. Context What does AI need to know before it can do the work well? (less is usually better) 2. Skill What is the repeatable process, sequence, or task? 3. Examples What does good output look like? 4. Quality bar How should the team judge whether the work is actually usable? (I like human checkpoints) Once those pieces are captured, you can add the self learning layer. When someone finds a better example, improves the skill, updates the context, or raises the quality bar, that improvement should feed back into a shared system. That is how the workflow gets better over time instead of staying trapped in one person’s setup. I’m teaching this process in a live class on June 18 at 11:00 AM PT: Build an AI Ready Knowledge System Your Team Can Share Comment “AI” and i’ll send you the invite. content: It takes a lot for AI to be useful for companies. Thats because most of the “how” to us AI for a task lives inside individual people’s heads. One person has the best prompt. Another person knows which context to include. Someone else knows what a good output should look like. And the company gets the benefit of the work, but the actual system behind the work is scattered across people, docs, tools, and personal setups. That creates a real problem. If the person changes roles, gets busy, or leaves, the workflow can disappear with them. The next step is making AI workflows company owned. That means capturing: - The context AI needs - The reusable skill or workflow - Examples of good output - The quality bar the team should use On June 18 at 11:00 AM PT, I’m hosting a live class on how to build an AI Ready Knowledge System your team can share. Comment “AI” and i’ll send you the invite. P.S. make sure we are connected so I can dm you.

content: Reps listen to coaching more when the data backs it up. If coaching sounds like opinion, reps can dismiss it. If coaching is tied to the numbers, it lands differently. Example: “Your next steps need work.” Easy to ignore. But this is harder to ignore: “Your deals are spending 12 days longer in stage 2 than the team average, and your calls are missing clear next steps with date, owner, and outcome.” Now the coaching is specific. Now the rep sees the impact. Now you can work on one behavior. That’s what this session is about. How to use AI to coach reps in a way that is clear, practical, and backed by the data. We’ll cover: → Which signals matter → How to pick the right skill to coach → How to show reps the impact → How to track whether coaching is working I’m breaking it down live on June 11 at 11am PT. content: Most sales coaching is based on what the manager notices. That’s a problem. Because managers are busy, miss things, and sometimes are just flat out wrong. And because of this reps hear feedback as opinion. So the rep thinks: “That was just one call.” Or... “That’s not what usually happens.” And now the coaching goes nowhere. Here’s what works better... Use data. CRM data tells you where performance is breaking. Call data tells you what behavior is causing it. Then coaching gets way more specific. Example: Low discovery to opportunity conversion Weak pain quantification on calls Coach better second and third layer pain questions Track if conversion improves That’s a real coaching system and AI can track all of that across all calls. I put together a simple framework for using sales data to coach reps. It helps you identify: → What skill needs work → Why it matters → What behavior to coach → How to track improvement I’m walking through the full system live on June 11. content: Sometimes AI outputs are amazing and other times its a mess. That is because the prompt is inconsistent. Vague input. Vague output. That’s it. If you ask AI to “write a follow-up email,” it has to guess everything (even if you give it a ton of context). Who the buyer is What happened on the call What matters to them What the next step is What tone to use So the output comes back generic. Here’s what works better... Give AI a real structure. Objective Context Inputs Constraints Examples Output format Now AI has something to work with. I put together a simple prompt framework your team can use for: → Account research → Call prep → Follow-up emails → Meeting recaps → Next steps I’m walking through the full framework live on June 4 with Sell Better. content: I've gotten 3,476,725 views on instagram over the last 30 days. The 30 days prior I got 71,782. Here is how I did it... 1/ I posted consistently Over the past few months I have really made an effort to post consistently. 2/ I looked at the data For every reel, I reviewed the data. Skip rate Like rate Share rate Save rate Based on that I reshot videos that seemed to have high like, share, and save rates but low skip rates. 3/ Built an AI agent to do that process for me Once I got the hang of doing this, I built an AI agent to analyze every video and tell me if I should re record it and/or what other topics I should make a video on. So every day I wake up to a report in slack that tells me exactly what content to create. This Thursday at 11am PT I am walking through how to build this system (and giving away all the prompts). content: Quick question for SaaS founders: When was the last time your marketing team built a piece of content based on what's actually happening on sales calls this week? If the answer is "never" or "I don't know"….you're not alone. Most marketing roadmaps get built once a quarter, in a planning meeting, based on what someone thinks the market wants. Meanwhile, your sales team is on calls every day - hearing the actual objections, the actual questions, the actual reasons deals stall. None of it makes it back to marketing. 🎯 On May 14th at 11AM PT, Dan McDermott is joining us to teach a two-way AI system that fixes this. Sales calls drive the marketing roadmap. Existing content becomes a library reps actually use. One workflow runs both. You'll walk away with: ✅ The 3 prompts Dan uses with his B2B SaaS clients ✅ A clear, actionable plan to level up your marketing ✅ A clear, actionable plan to enable your sales reps …all using material you already have.
